title: Leaders, gainers and unexpected winners in the Enterprise AI arms race
body: Survey of enterprise CIOs on AI model adoption and spending
Data covering production usage, testing, and wallet share across frontier labs

title: Agenda: Enterprise AI Landscape 2025
body: Market leaders and adoption rates
Anthropic's rapid growth trajectory
Enterprise spending trends and projections
Multi-model strategy prevalence
Latest model version adoption patterns
Category leaders in chat and coding
Incumbent advantage in enterprise decisions

title: OpenAI Dominates Production Deployment
body: 78% of surveyed enterprise CIOs are using OpenAI models in production
OpenAI commands ~56% wallet share across enterprise AI spending
Maintains clear leadership position in enterprise market penetration

title: Anthropic: Fastest Growing Frontier Lab
body: Since May 2025, Anthropic posted the largest share increase of any frontier lab
Growing 25% in enterprise penetration over this period
Represents the most significant market share shift in the competitive landscape

title: Anthropic's Current Market Position
body: 44% of enterprises now use Anthropic in production
Rising to over 63% including testing environments
Demonstrates strong momentum from testing to production deployment

title: Enterprise AI Spending: Two-Year Growth
body: Over the last 2 years, average enterprise AI spend on LLMs rose from ~$4.5M to ~$7M
Represents 55% growth in spending over the two-year period
Reflects increasing commitment to AI infrastructure at enterprise scale

title: Projected Spending Acceleration
body: Enterprises expecting ~65% growth to ~$11.6M this year
Growth rate accelerating compared to previous two-year period
Budget increases signal confidence in AI ROI and strategic importance

title: Multi-Model Strategy Becomes Standard
body: 81% now use three or more model families
Up from 68% less than a year ago
13 percentage point increase demonstrates rapid adoption of portfolio approach

title: Anthropic Leads in Latest Model Adoption
body: 75% of Anthropic customers had Sonnet 4.5 or Opus 4.5 in production
Significantly higher adoption rate of newest model versions
Suggests strong customer satisfaction and upgrade velocity

title: OpenAI's Latest Model Adoption Lags
body: 46% of OpenAI's customers on GPT 5.2 or 5.2 Pro in production
29 percentage point gap compared to Anthropic's latest model adoption
May indicate slower upgrade cycles or greater satisfaction with previous versions

title: Microsoft 365 Copilot Leads Enterprise Chat
body: Microsoft 365 Copilot leads enterprise chat category
Leverages Microsoft's existing enterprise footprint and Office integration
Demonstrates advantage of embedding AI into existing workflow tools

title: GitHub Copilot: Coding Category Leader
body: GitHub Copilot remains the coding leader
Maintains dominant position in developer-focused AI tools
Benefits from GitHub's established developer platform and community

title: Incumbent Advantage Drives Enterprise Decisions
body: 65% of enterprises preferred incumbent solutions
Citing trust and integration as primary decision factors
Established vendor relationships and existing infrastructure integrations outweigh pure performance considerations

title: Key Takeaways: Enterprise AI Market Dynamics
body: OpenAI leads but Anthropic shows strongest growth momentum
Enterprise spending accelerating with multi-model strategies becoming standard
Trust and integration favor incumbents despite newer entrants' technical advances
Latest model adoption rates vary significantly by vendor
